original_query: select  min(i_item_id)  ,min(i_item_desc)  ,min(s_store_id)  ,min(s_store_name)  ,min(ss_net_profit)  ,min(sr_net_loss)  ,min(cs_net_profit)  ,min(ss_item_sk)  ,min(sr_ticket_number)  ,min(cs_order_number)  from  store_sales  ,store_returns  ,catalog_sales  ,date_dim d1  ,date_dim d2  ,date_dim d3  ,store  ,item  where  d1.d_moy = 6  and d1.d_year = 2000  and d1.d_date_sk = ss_sold_date_sk  and i_item_sk = ss_item_sk  and s_store_sk = ss_store_sk  and ss_customer_sk = sr_customer_sk  and ss_item_sk = sr_item_sk  and ss_ticket_number = sr_ticket_number  and sr_returned_date_sk = d2.d_date_sk  and d2.d_moy               between 6 and  6 + 2  and d2.d_year              = 2000  and sr_customer_sk = cs_bill_customer_sk  and sr_item_sk = cs_item_sk  and cs_sold_date_sk = d3.d_date_sk  and d3.d_moy               between 6 and  6 + 2  and d3.d_year              = 2000  ;
rewritten_query:
SELECT 
    MIN(i_item_id), 
    MIN(i_item_desc), 
    MIN(s_store_id), 
    MIN(s_store_name), 
    MIN(ss_net_profit), 
    MIN(sr_net_loss), 
    MIN(cs_net_profit), 
    MIN(ss_item_sk), 
    MIN(sr_ticket_number), 
    MIN(cs_order_number) 
FROM 
    store_sales, 
    store_returns, 
    catalog_sales, 
    date_dim d1, 
    date_dim d2, 
    date_dim d3, 
    store, 
    item 
WHERE 
    d1.d_moy = 6 
    AND d1.d_year = 2000 
    AND d1.d_date_sk = ss_sold_date_sk 
    AND i_item_sk = ss_item_sk 
    AND s_store_sk = ss_store_sk 
    AND ss_customer_sk = sr_customer_sk 
    AND ss_item_sk = sr_item_sk 
    AND ss_ticket_number = sr_ticket_number 
    AND sr_returned_date_sk = d2.d_date_sk 
    AND d2.d_moy BETWEEN 6 AND 6 + 2 
    AND d2.d_year = 2000 
    AND sr_customer_sk = cs_bill_customer_sk 
    AND sr_item_sk = cs_item_sk 
    AND cs_sold_date_sk = d3.d_date_sk 
    AND d3.d_moy BETWEEN 6 AND 6 + 2 
    AND d3.d_year = 2000;

rewrite_suggestion.nave_suggestion: Use CTEs to pre-filter the large fact tables (store_sales, store_returns, catalog_sales) with date conditions before joining them. This reduces the amount of data processed in the joins.

Extract the date filtering conditions into separate CTEs (d2_filter and d3_filter) to avoid redundant date dimension scans since both have the same condition (d_moy BETWEEN 6 AND 8 AND d_year = 2000).
Change the join order to start with filtered store_sales (ss) and then join to the other tables, which may reduce the number of rows processed in the early stages of the query execution.

original_query: select  min(i_item_id)  ,min(i_item_desc)  ,min(s_store_id)  ,min(s_store_name)  ,min(ss_net_profit)  ,min(sr_net_loss)  ,min(cs_net_profit)  ,min(ss_item_sk)  ,min(sr_ticket_number)  ,min(cs_order_number)  from  store_sales  ,store_returns  ,catalog_sales  ,date_dim d1  ,date_dim d2  ,date_dim d3  ,store  ,item  where  d1.d_moy = 9  and d1.d_year = 1999  and d1.d_date_sk = ss_sold_date_sk  and i_item_sk = ss_item_sk  and s_store_sk = ss_store_sk  and ss_customer_sk = sr_customer_sk  and ss_item_sk = sr_item_sk  and ss_ticket_number = sr_ticket_number  and sr_returned_date_sk = d2.d_date_sk  and d2.d_moy               between 9 and  9 + 2  and d2.d_year              = 1999  and sr_customer_sk = cs_bill_customer_sk  and sr_item_sk = cs_item_sk  and cs_sold_date_sk = d3.d_date_sk  and d3.d_moy               between 9 and  9 + 2  and d3.d_year              = 1999  ;
rewritten_query:
SELECT 
  MIN(i_item_id),
  MIN(i_item_desc),
  MIN(s_store_id),
  MIN(s_store_name),
  MIN(ss_net_profit),
  MIN(sr_net_loss),
  MIN(cs_net_profit),
  MIN(ss_item_sk),
  MIN(sr_ticket_number),
  MIN(cs_order_number)
FROM 
  store_sales
  JOIN date_dim d1 ON d1.d_date_sk = ss_sold_date_sk AND d1.d_moy = 9 AND d1.d_year = 1999
  JOIN store ON s_store_sk = ss_store_sk
  JOIN item ON i_item_sk = ss_item_sk
  JOIN store_returns ON ss_customer_sk = sr_customer_sk 
    AND ss_item_sk = sr_item_sk 
    AND ss_ticket_number = sr_ticket_number
  JOIN date_dim d2 ON sr_returned_date_sk = d2.d_date_sk 
    AND d2.d_moy BETWEEN 9 AND 11 
    AND d2.d_year = 1999
  JOIN catalog_sales ON sr_customer_sk = cs_bill_customer_sk 
    AND sr_item_sk = cs_item_sk
  JOIN date_dim d3 ON cs_sold_date_sk = d3.d_date_sk 
    AND d3.d_moy BETWEEN 9 AND 11 
    AND d3.d_year = 1999;

rewrite_suggestion.nave_suggestion: The analysis suggests that the original SQL query is already well-optimized. The report considered using CTEs to pre-filter date dimensions and changing join order, but found that these approaches might not improve performance. The nested loops in the execution plan appear appropriate as they operate on indexed columns, and the database is already leveraging parallelism for large scans.

original_query: select  min(i_item_id)  ,min(i_item_desc)  ,min(s_store_id)  ,min(s_store_name)  ,min(ss_net_profit)  ,min(sr_net_loss)  ,min(cs_net_profit)  ,min(ss_item_sk)  ,min(sr_ticket_number)  ,min(cs_order_number)  from  store_sales  ,store_returns  ,catalog_sales  ,date_dim d1  ,date_dim d2  ,date_dim d3  ,store  ,item  where  d1.d_moy = 6  and d1.d_year = 2002  and d1.d_date_sk = ss_sold_date_sk  and i_item_sk = ss_item_sk  and s_store_sk = ss_store_sk  and ss_customer_sk = sr_customer_sk  and ss_item_sk = sr_item_sk  and ss_ticket_number = sr_ticket_number  and sr_returned_date_sk = d2.d_date_sk  and d2.d_moy               between 6 and  6 + 2  and d2.d_year              = 2002  and sr_customer_sk = cs_bill_customer_sk  and sr_item_sk = cs_item_sk  and cs_sold_date_sk = d3.d_date_sk  and d3.d_moy               between 6 and  6 + 2  and d3.d_year              = 2002  ;
rewritten_query:
WITH filtered_sales AS (
    SELECT ss.*
    FROM store_sales ss
    JOIN date_dim d1 ON ss.ss_sold_date_sk = d1.d_date_sk
    WHERE d1.d_moy = 6 AND d1.d_year = 2002
),
filtered_returns AS (
    SELECT sr.*
    FROM store_returns sr
    JOIN date_dim d2 ON sr.sr_returned_date_sk = d2.d_date_sk
    WHERE d2.d_moy BETWEEN 6 AND 8 AND d2.d_year = 2002
),
filtered_catalog AS (
    SELECT cs.*
    FROM catalog_sales cs
    JOIN date_dim d3 ON cs.cs_sold_date_sk = d3.d_date_sk
    WHERE d3.d_moy BETWEEN 6 AND 8 AND d3.d_year = 2002
)
SELECT 
    min(i_item_id),
    min(i_item_desc),
    min(s_store_id),
    min(s_store_name),
    min(ss_net_profit),
    min(sr_net_loss),
    min(cs_net_profit),
    min(ss_item_sk),
    min(sr_ticket_number),
    min(cs_order_number)
FROM filtered_sales ss
JOIN filtered_returns sr
    ON ss.ss_customer_sk = sr.sr_customer_sk
    AND ss.ss_item_sk = sr.sr_item_sk
    AND ss.ss_ticket_number = sr.sr_ticket_number
JOIN filtered_catalog cs
    ON sr.sr_customer_sk = cs.cs_bill_customer_sk
    AND sr.sr_item_sk = cs.cs_item_sk
JOIN store s ON ss.ss_store_sk = s.s_store_sk
JOIN item i ON ss.ss_item_sk = i.i_item_sk;

rewrite_suggestion.nave_suggestion: Use subqueries to filter large fact tables (store_sales, store_returns, catalog_sales) with their respective date conditions before joining them together. This pushes down predicates earlier in the execution plan, reducing the number of rows processed during the joins.
